sourceLabel: DNA: urgent safety warning with contextual locator
patternSummary: Urgent hook in caps -> witness detail -> corroborating authority -> escalating concrete claim -> contextual locator -> firsthand-experience question; reuse only with verified facts.
conflict: promised treatment versus suspected substitution or unsafe practice
style: high-energy colloquial, caps for emphasis, slang, direct address, expressive emoji; fact-check sensitive claims
informationGap: what actually happened and whether other people had the same experience
discussionPattern: give a specific warning, then invite firsthand reports without asserting unverified facts
ctaStyle: contextual locator with explicit name, followed by an experience question

sourceLabel: DNA: low-budget coffee hack with visual proof
patternSummary: Playful confession or apology -> concrete visual proof -> reveal the non-negotiable texture step -> numbered ingredients and alternatives -> practical search caveat.
conflict: recreating cafe texture while keeping the recipe affordable
persona: enthusiastic home barista sharing a tested shortcut
style: casual, playful, concise, visual-first, low-budget, emoji-led emphasis
informationGap: which small step makes the coffee creamy instead of ordinary
discussionPattern: show the result, teach the exact sequence, then invite substitutions or adaptations
ctaStyle: numbered ingredient references with alternatives, never a bare sales CTA
